behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the file path, function (upgrade), commit message literal, --message/--no-verify flags, console_print, and 'auto-fixes' substring mentioned in tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes expected behavior (catch error, re-stage, retry with --no-verify) without providing code; agent must figure out try/except structure.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for structural elements (CalledProcessError handler, git add, git commit, --no-verify, --message, console_print) that any correct fix would include.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use ast parsing to inspect structure and py_compile/black for execution checks, rather than plain text substring grep.

test_not_tautological.reason: Pass-to-pass checks (syntax, subprocess import, black) plus behavioral AST checks require actual implementation of retry logic.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover try/except, console_print, git add re-stage, --no-verify retry, --message flag, same commit message — matching all requirements in instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (PASS_TO_PASS, BEHAVIORAL TESTS) and descriptive names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code modification, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names (ci_commands.py, upgrade, console_print) are consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to the file and function but doesn't give line numbers or exact code; localization is reasonable given the bug report context.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, not a config edit.
